rag_rules: Place input envelope assumptions in component type annexes when they refer to component ports and use visible feature names or concrete bounds.
Use valid AGREE syntax for assumptions and guarantees, ensuring type compatibility and scope alignment with AADL component structures.
Generate AGREE annex blocks inside `annex agree {** ... **};` and place them in appropriate component type or implementation scopes based on the requirement and model structure.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all identifiers and types are present in the AADL model and avoid introducing undefined or package-qualified references.
Use constants and equations to express numeric ranges and boolean conditions, aligning with the types and features visible in the AADL model.
Avoid assumptions or guarantees that reference non-numeric or non-visible data unless explicitly supported by the AADL model structure.
Do not invent bounds or constraints that are not directly stated in the requirement or implied by the AADL data types and ports.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and placed in valid AADL component type or implementation scopes.
Assumptions and guarantees should use valid AGREE expressions with matching types (int, real, bool) and visible identifiers from the AADL model.
Constants and equations in AGREE should be declared with correct scalar types and must not reference undefined or non-existent AADL properties.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes unless they are represented through valid variables or outputs.
Do not assume that event ports or opaque data types can be directly compared as numeric or Boolean values in AGREE.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or AADL property names as AGREE variables unless they are explicitly exposed in the model.
irrelevant_topics: Fusion of multiple models or annexes
